tool_definitions.name: TodoWrite
tool_definitions.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tool_definitions.name: EnterPlanMode
tool_definitions.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tool_definitions.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_definitions.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan. Pass your plan as plan_content — it is required and must be non-empty.

tool_definitions.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_definitions.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tool_definitions.name: Glob
tool_definitions.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tool_definitions.name: Grep
tool_definitions.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tool_definitions.name: ToolSearch
tool_definitions.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tool_definitions.name: task_create
tool_definitions.description: Create a new task on the task board. Returns the created task. Use task_list to see all tasks, task_update to mark progress.

tool_definitions.name: task_delegate
tool_definitions.description: Delegate a pending task to a named child agent. Records the intended executor in metadata.delegatedTo only — the task remains pending with no assignedTo change. The spawned worker claims the task via task_update(status: in_progress).

tool_definitions.name: agent_spawn
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a named child agent to complete a task. Returns the child agent's final output as a string.

steps.message: MCP transport: connecting
MCP transport: connected

You are a coordinator. Execute ALL 11 steps below in order. Do not skip any step.
1. Call AskUserQuestion with ONE question: 'Which refactoring scope?' options: 'Targeted — one module only', 'Full — all modules'.
2. Call EnterPlanMode.
3. Call TodoWrite: [{id:'explore',content:'Explore repo',status:'in_progress'}, {id:'write-plan',content:'Write plan',status:'pending'}, {id:'dispatch',content:'Spawn refactor worker',status:'pending'}].
4. Call Glob pattern='*.json'.
5. Call TodoWrite: explore=completed, write-plan=in_progress, dispatch=pending.
6. Call TodoWrite: explore=completed, write-plan=completed, dispatch=in_progress.
7. Call ExitPlanMode.
8. Call task_create: subject='Refactor targeted module', description='Apply approved plan'. NOTE the returned task_id.
9. Call task_delegate: task_id=<from step 8>, agent_id='refactor-worker'. THIS STEP IS MANDATORY before agent_spawn.
10. Call agent_spawn: agent_name='refactor-worker', description='Refactor targeted module'.
11. Call TodoWrite: dispatch=completed (this clears the list).
Report: user choice, worker output, list cleared confirmation.

results.content:
I'll execute all 11 steps in order. Let's begin!

---

**Step 1: Ask the user about refactoring scope**

tools.name: TodoWrite
tools.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tools.name: EnterPlanMode
tools.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tools.name: ExitPlanMode
tools.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan. Pass your plan as plan_content — it is required and must be non-empty.

tools.name: AskUserQuestion
tools.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tools.name: ToolSearch
tools.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tools.name: task_create
tools.description: Create a new task on the task board. Returns the created task. Use task_list to see all tasks, task_update to mark progress.

tools.name: task_delegate
tools.description: Delegate a pending task to a named child agent. Records the intended executor in metadata.delegatedTo only — the task remains pending with no assignedTo change. The spawned worker claims the task via task_update(status: in_progress).

tools.name: TodoWrite
tools.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tools.name: EnterPlanMode
tools.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tools.name: ExitPlanMode
tools.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan. Pass your plan as plan_content — it is required and must be non-empty.

tools.name: AskUserQuestion
tools.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tools.name: ToolSearch
tools.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tools.name: task_create
tools.description: Create a new task on the task board. Returns the created task. Use task_list to see all tasks, task_update to mark progress.

tools.name: task_delegate
tools.description: Delegate a pending task to a named child agent. Records the intended executor in metadata.delegatedTo only — the task remains pending with no assignedTo change. The spawned worker claims the task via task_update(status: in_progress).

tools.name: agent_spawn
tools.description: Spawn a named child agent to complete a task. Returns the child agent's final output as a string.

arguments.plan_content:
## Refactoring Plan — Targeted (One Module)

**User Selection:** Targeted — one module only

**Repo Exploration Findings:**
- Root-level JSON files found: `tsconfig.base.json`, `turbo.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `package.json`, `biome.json`
- This appears to be a TypeScript monorepo using Turborepo, with Biome for linting/formatting.

**Plan Steps:**
1. Identify the single target module to refactor (highest-priority or most-changed module).
2. Apply refactoring best practices to that module only: clean up imports, improve naming consistency, enforce Biome formatting rules, and align with `tsconfig.base.json` strict settings.
3. Run Biome lint + format on the targeted module to validate changes.
4. Verify TypeScript compilation for the targeted module passes with no errors.
5. Submit a concise diff/summary of changes for review.

**Out of Scope:** All other modules remain untouched.

5. Submit a concise diff/summary of changes for review.

**Out of Scope:** All other modules remain untouched."}

tools.name: TodoWrite
tools.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tools.name: EnterPlanMode
tools.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tools.name: ExitPlanMode
tools.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan. Pass your plan as plan_content — it is required and must be non-empty.

tools.name: AskUserQuestion
tools.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tools.name: ToolSearch
tools.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tools.name: task_create
tools.description: Create a new task on the task board. Returns the created task. Use task_list to see all tasks, task_update to mark progress.

tools.name: task_delegate
tools.description: Delegate a pending task to a named child agent. Records the intended executor in metadata.delegatedTo only — the task remains pending with no assignedTo change. The spawned worker claims the task via task_update(status: in_progress).

tools.name: agent_spawn
tools.description: Spawn a named child agent to complete a task. Returns the child agent's final output as a string.

results.content:
---

## ✅ All 11 Steps Complete — Final Report

| # | Step | Result |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | **AskUserQuestion** | ✅ Asked scope question |
| 2 | **EnterPlanMode** | ✅ Entered plan mode |
| 3 | **TodoWrite (initial)** | ✅ List created: `explore` in_progress, `write-plan` + `dispatch` pending |
| 4 | **Glob `*.json`** | ✅ Found: `tsconfig.base.json`, `turbo.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `package.json`, `biome.json` |
| 5 | **TodoWrite (explore done)** | ✅ `explore` → completed, `write-plan` → in_progress |
| 6 | **TodoWrite (plan done)** | ✅ `write-plan` → completed, `dispatch` → in_progress |
| 7 | **ExitPlanMode** | ✅ Plan approved |
| 8 | **task_create** | ✅ Created `task_3`: "Refactor targeted module" |
| 9 | **task_delegate** | ✅ Delegated `task_3` → `refactor-worker` |
| 10 | **agent_spawn** | ✅ Worker spawned and completed |
| 11 | **TodoWrite (dispatch done)** | ✅ All todos completed → **list cleared** |

---

### 📋 Summary

- **User Choice:** `Targeted — one module only`
- **Worker Output:** `"Task delegated to refactor-worker: Refactor targeted module — result: done"`
- **Todo List:** ✅ Cleared (all 3 items reached `completed` status, list auto-cleared confirmed)
